name: Mel Brooks
blurb: The 2000 Year Old Man — a five-album improv duet with Carl Reiner that is the foundational pre-modern stand-up record, half a millennium of Jewish-grandfather wisdom answered into Reiner's straight-man microphone.
bio: Mel Brooks's filmmaking — The Producers, Blazing Saddles, Young Frankenstein, Spaceballs — is the front door, but the 2000 Year Old Man is the room where pre-modern American stand-up actually lives. Beginning in the late 1950s as a party piece for Sid Caesar's writers' room — Reiner as the tape-recorder-equipped straight man, Brooks improvising as a 2,013-year-old Jewish witness to the crucifixion, the Roman Empire, and his 42,000 unvisitable children — the routine became five Capitol/Warner/Rhino albums released 1960–1997, an Ed Sullivan Show debut in February 1961, an animated CBS special in 1975, and a Grammy Award for Best Spoken Comedy Album in 1998. The first album was added to the Library of Congress's National Recording Registry in 2008. Brooks is one of fewer than twenty EGOT winners; the Old Man is the comedy bit on which the rest is built. Reiner is the foil; Brooks is the comic.

timeline.ev: Born Melvin James Kaminsky on June 28 in Brooklyn, New York
Drafted into the US Army; serves as a combat engineer in Europe in WWII
Sid Caesar hires him to write for the DuMont/NBC series The Admiral Broadway Revue; later Your Show of Shows
2000 Years with Carl Reiner and Mel Brooks released (Capitol) — first 2000 Year Old Man album
TV debut of the 2000 Year Old Man on The Ed Sullivan Show, February 12
The Producers — directorial debut wins Academy Award for Best Original Screenplay
Blazing Saddles and Young Frankenstein — the second- and third-highest-grossing US films of the year
Animated CBS special The 2000 Year Old Man premieres January 11
The 2000 Year Old Man in the Year 2000 album with Reiner — wins the 1998 Grammy for Best Spoken Comedy Album
The Producers (Broadway musical) wins a record 12 Tony Awards — completes EGOT
2000 Years with Carl Reiner and Mel Brooks inducted into the Library of Congress National Recording Registry

accolades: EGOT winner (Emmy, Grammy, Oscar, Tony) — one of fewer than twenty in history
Grammy — Best Spoken Comedy Album, The 2000 Year Old Man in the Year 2000 (1998)
Academy Award — Best Original Screenplay, The Producers (1968)
Kennedy Center Honors (2009); AFI Life Achievement Award (2013); National Medal of Arts (2016)
Library of Congress National Recording Registry — 2000 Years with Carl Reiner and Mel Brooks (2008)
Tony Awards — Best Musical, Book, Score for The Producers (2001, set the all-time record at 12 wins)

ranking_notes: Edge-case: the canonical Brooks 'bit' is the 2000 Year Old Man — a duet with Reiner spread across five albums and multiple TV appearances. Treat the routine as the single ranked artifact rather than splitting it into per-album fragments. The 1961 Ed Sullivan debut and the 1975 animated special are the watchable instances; the recorded albums are where the comedy actually lives.
